member.name: Marie Goldman
member.synopsis: Marie Goldman is the Liberal Democrat MP for Chelmsford , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024. She currently undertakes the role of Liberal Democrat Spokesperson (Women and Equalities).

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.

writtenQuestions.heading: Nutrition
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, when his Department plans to implement mandatory health targets for large food businesses.
writtenQuestions.answerText: As set out in the 10-Year Health Plan, by the end of this Parliament, we will introduce mandatory healthier food sales reporting for all large companies in the sector. The plan also committed to introduce new targets to increase the healthiness of sales, ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Mid and South Essex NHS Foundation Trust: Standards
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment he has made of the effectiveness of the NHS Intensive Recovery Support Programme on the performance of Mid and South Essex NHS Foundation Trust.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The NHS Intensive Recovery Programme is focused on bringing decisive action to address longstanding issues, structural constraints and financial imbalance, in a small number of trusts with significant challenges. The first wave of trusts in the programme ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Pupils: Drinks
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what steps her Department is taking to (a) reduce pupils’ access to sugary drinks and (b) support provision of healthier drink options throughout the school day.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The School Food Standards set the mandatory nutritional framework for food and drink provided in state‑funded schools in England. Since the Standards were introduced in 2014, the dietary recommendations on free sugar, fibre and sweeteners have changed and...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Education

writtenQuestions.heading: Occupied Territories: Women's Rights
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, what steps she is taking to provide (a) financial and (b) diplomatic support to women's rights organisations operating in Gaza and the Occupied Palestinian Territories.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The UK advocates for the full, equal and meaningful and safe participation of women in political and peace processes in Palestine, including through support to women's rights organisations. Through programmes such as Tasdeer, the UK supports women's econo...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Autoimmune Diseases: Drugs
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps he is taking to help tackle medication shortages, particularly those for (a) auto-immune and (b) muscle weakness conditions.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Medicine supply chains are complex, global, and highly regulated and there are a number of reasons why supply can be disrupted, many of which are not specific to the United Kingdom and outside of Government control, including manufacturing difficulties, a...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Probation Service: Neurodiversity
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, whether the Probation Service is continuing to fund neurodiversity specialist services.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Neurodiversity specialist services are arranged by probation regions individually, based on the specific needs of people on probation in the region. Arrangements therefore vary in terms of type of provision and duration.Integrated Care Boards are responsi...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Justice

writtenQuestions.heading: Construction: Apprentices
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what recent assessment he has made of the (a) availability and (b) affordability of transport options for apprentices working on remote construction sites.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Support for apprentices travelling to sites is provided through employer arrangements including employer-organised transport or reimbursement of travel expenses. On more remote sites accommodation may be provided. The JIB Industrial Determination 2026-202...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Business and Trade

writtenQuestions.heading: Gaza: Health Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, (a) how many and (b) what proportion of healthcare facilities in Gaza in receipt of UK aid have been attacked since 7 October 2023.
writtenQuestions.answerText: It is estimated that 90 per cent of all hospitals and primary care facilities have been damaged in Gaza, with less than half now functional and no hospital currently operational in North Gaza.We have consistently urged the Israeli authorities to avoid thi...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Visas: Skilled Workers
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, with reference to the Answer of 14 April 2026 to Question 124428 on Visas: Skilled Workers, what steps she is taking to review the oversubscription of priority services for changes of visa sponsorship
writtenQuestions.answerText: All straightforward skilled worker visa applications are being processed within our advertised service standard. If customers wish to receive a quicker response, they have the option of selecting a priority service on which there is no cap.With regards to...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Home Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Visas: Skilled Workers
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what steps her Department is taking to reduce waiting times for skilled worker visa applications and renewals.
writtenQuestions.answerText: All straightforward skilled worker visa applications are being processed within our advertised service standard. If customers wish to receive a quicker response, they have the option of selecting a priority service on which there is no cap.With regards to...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Home Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Israel: Gender Based Violence
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, with reference to the Answer to Question UIN 87730 of 11 November 2025, if her Department will publish a formal response to the United Nations Independent International Commi
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the Hon Member to the answer provided on 15 April 2025 to HL6110.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Parking: Pedestrian Areas
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, pursuant to her Written Statement on 8 January 2026, by which date her Department will introduce secondary legislation to enable local authorities to conduct civil enforcement of unnecessary pavement obstructio
writtenQuestions.answerText: It is not possible to specify the date the Statutory Instrument will be laid before Parliament. It is our intention to lay before Parliament by the end of the year.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Transport

writtenQuestions.heading: NHS
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps his Department is taking to undertake the statutory ten-year review of the NHS Constitution.
writtenQuestions.answerText: A consultation on the NHS Constitution as part of a 10-year review was in May 2024 and was superseded by the General Election in July 2024. For this reason, the 10-year review is now closed.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Health Services: LGBT+ People
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, with reference to the Answer of 5 January 2026 to question 97838, what his planned timetable is for the publication of the LGBT+ health evidence review.
writtenQuestions.answerText: NHS England launched the LGBT+ Health Evidence Review in July 2025, and it is being led by Dr Michael Brady, the NHS England National Adviser for LGBT+ Health. Good progress has been made on the Review, including a literature review, analysis of relevant ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Pupils: Attendance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what steps she is taking to address the rising rates in severe absence among girls in schools.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Absence remains a major barrier to children’s opportunity. To achieve and thrive, pupils need to be in school regularly. Thanks to the efforts of schools, trusts, local authorities and wider partners, attendance improved significantly in 2024/25, with chi...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Education
